sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check blue position after 4 UPs total
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Blue: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
        break

# Check red blocks for alignment
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'R':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Red: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

If I move DOWN one time, the blue would be at rows 24-27, which aligns with the right red block vertically.

Let me try a systematic approach: move the blue to align with the right red block (rows 24-27, cols 28-39) and press SPACE. To do this:

- Move LEFT 1: cols 36-55 -> 32-51
- Move LEFT 1: cols 32-51 -> 28-47
- Press SPACE

# Right red block at rows 24-27, cols 28-39
# Move DOWN 1 to align vertically with right red block
# Move LEFT 2 to align horizontally with right red block (cols 28-47)
# Then press SPACE

</parameter>
